item.title: Varese hospitals: fewer beds, more specialization in 20 years
item.description: Over the past two decades, the number of beds in Varese hospitals has decreased by 30-40%, with a more efficient but also more fragile care model.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 30-40% fewer beds in 20 years
- More efficient but fragile care model
- Specialization of facilities

## Key facts
- **What**: Reduction in beds and specialization
- When: Over the past twenty years
- Where: Hospitals in Varese, Busto Arsizio, Gallarate
- Who: ASST Sette Laghi, ASST Valle Olona
- Amount: 30-40% reduction in beds

Over the past twenty years, the number of beds in hospitals in the area between Varese, Busto Arsizio, and Gallarate has significantly decreased. However, this is not just about cuts: there is a real revolution in the care model, which has changed the face of facilities such as the Circolo in Varese, Del Ponte, Busto Arsizio Hospital, and Sant’Antonio Abate in Gallarate. A change that has made the system more efficient but, according to some operators, also more fragile.

The starting figure is eloquent: in the 2000s, Lombardy had an average of five to six beds per thousand inhabitants. Today, that number has dropped to between three and 3.2, with a reduction of around 30-40 percent. A trend that has affected all hospitals in the Varesotto area, albeit with different methods and intensity depending on the role each facility plays in the provincial network.

### The Circolo in Varese: the hub aiming high

The Circolo Hospital is the provincial reference for high specialties and the most complex cases. Its current capacity reflects this mission: around a hundred general medicine beds, about thirty cardiology beds with an attached coronary unit, thirty neurology beds, about twenty respectively for oncology, nephrology, and vascular surgery. In surgery, the facility maintains around sixty beds for general surgery, about fifty for orthopedics and traumatology, fifteen for neurosurgery. The critical area has about twenty intensive care beds and around fifteen sub-intensive care beds.

Over the years, the Circolo has progressively reduced the beds in traditional medicine and surgery, but has strengthened high-intensity areas. The result is a facility with an enhanced role, not diminished, although profoundly different from the past. The day hospital has become a pillar: oncology, cardiology, hematology, and nephrology now manage much of the activity on an outpatient or day hospitalization basis, reserving ordinary beds for the most complex cases.

### Del Ponte: the exception to the trend

The Filippo Del Ponte Hospital in Varese is a case apart. While elsewhere the number of beds is decreasing, here the trend has gone in the opposite direction, at least in some sectors. Del Ponte is a predominantly maternal and child facility, and its specific mission has preserved it — and to some extent enhanced it — from the deepest cuts. Obstetrics and gynecology now have almost seventy-two active beds, with four places in day hospital. Neonatology has eighteen, neonatal intensive care eight, pediatric surgery seven with as many as eight day hospital places. Child neuropsychiatry has ten ordinary beds and two in day hospital. Even oncology is present, but only in day hospital, with three places, indicating that the outpatient model is also gaining ground in this facility.

### Busto Arsizio: an advanced spoke

The hospital in Busto Arsizio plays the role of an advanced spoke in the Lombard network, with a wide clinical coverage but lower complexity compared to the Varese hub. Its current capacity is significant: twenty-six beds in obstetrics and gynecology with three places in day hospital, fourteen in hematology, sixteen in pediatrics, six in intensive care, fourteen in psychiatry with one place in day hospital, sixteen in nephrology, twelve or thirteen in psychiatry. Pulmonology has been active for eleven months ### Implications for patients and the healthcare system

The reduction in hospital beds and the specialization of facilities have led to a more efficient care model, but also to a more fragile system. Specialization has allowed resources to be concentrated in high-intensity areas, improving the quality of care for the most complex patients. However, this transformation has also made the system more vulnerable to peaks in demand or health crises, as demonstrated during the COVID-19 pandemic.

### Comparison with the previous situation

In the 2000s, hospitals in the Varesotto region had a larger number of beds and less specialization. This model allowed for greater flexibility in managing different types of patients, but with a lower quality of care compared to today. The reduction in beds and the increase in specialization have led to greater efficiency, but also to a greater dependence on specialized facilities for managing complex cases.

### Future scenarios

The future of the Varesotto healthcare system will depend on the ability to balance efficiency and resilience. It will be crucial to invest in staff training and technology to ensure a high-quality service, even in emergency situations. Furthermore, it will be necessary to strengthen collaboration between different facilities to optimize the use of resources and ensure fair and accessible healthcare for all citizens.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to access specialized care

To access specialized care in the hospitals of Varesotto, it is necessary to follow some specific procedures. First of all, it is important to consult your general practitioner, who can assess the need for a specialist visit and provide a referral to the most appropriate facility. In some cases, it may be necessary to obtain an appointment through the online or telephone booking system of ASST Sette Laghi and Valle Olona.

### Use the calculator for healthcare costs

To plan healthcare costs and better understand the expenses associated with specialized care, you can use the [healthcare cost calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to estimate the costs of specialist visits, hospital stays, and therapies, providing a clear and detailed overview of the expected expenses.

### Compare healthcare facilities

To compare the different healthcare facilities in Varesotto and choose the one that best suits your needs, you can use the [healthcare facility comparator](nav:health). This tool allows you to evaluate the services offered, patient reviews, and available specializations, making it easier to choose the most appropriate facility.

### Conclusion

The transformation of the hospitals in Varesotto over the last twenty years has led to a more efficient and specialized care model, but also to a more fragile system. To ensure high-quality healthcare, it is essential to follow the correct procedures to access specialized care and use the available tools to plan healthcare costs and compare facilities.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/meno-letti-piu-specializzazione-come-sono-cambiati-gli-ospedali-varesini-negli-ultimi-ventanni/2560024/)*

item.title: Varese energy costs hit businesses, cross-border workers
item.description: A small metalworking company pays 30% more for electricity than the European average. Analysis of the economic impacts.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Metalworking company pays +30% electricity vs EU average
- €285,000/year difference vs France
- Bank of Italy: risk of stagflation in 2027

## Key facts
- **What**: Rising energy costs in Varese province
- When: For four years, peaking in 2026
- Where: Varese province, Italy
- Who: Metalworking companies, Confartigianato Varese
- Amount: +30% on electricity cost vs EU average

A small metalworking company in Varese province pays 30% more for electricity than the European average. This is what emerges from a survey by Confartigianato Varese, which analyzed the impact of high energy costs not only on local businesses but on the entire territory's economy. In figures, for a company with a hundred employees, this translates to nearly three hundred thousand euros in additional costs every year. That money doesn't come from nowhere: it impacts jobs, consumer prices, and municipal revenues.

Imagine any warehouse in the outskirts of Busto Arsizio. A hundred employees, precision mechanical processing, electric furnaces running five days a week, an order worth 45% of turnover that leaves every month for a German client. This is the average profile of hundreds of companies in the area. That warehouse, in 2025, paid approximately €285,000 more in electricity than an identical warehouse, with the same installed power and consumption, located in the Lyon region. It's not an opinion, it's a calculation. Eurostat data on industrial prices for the first half of 2025 place Italy at €278 per megawatt-hour against France's €183, Germany's €242, Spain's €171, and a European average of €216. For a company that consumes three gigawatt-hours a year — an average figure for Varese's precision metalworking sector — the difference with France is exactly that amount: €285,000 in a solar year. This sum alone is worth the annual salary of seven or eight workers.

### Impact on the labor market
The increase in energy costs has a direct impact on the labor market. Companies, in fact, cannot fully absorb these costs without reducing profit margins or increasing product prices. This can lead to a reduction in investments in machinery and technology, limiting the competitiveness of local businesses. Furthermore, the additional cost of energy can reduce companies' ability to offer production bonuses or wage increases, directly affecting workers' purchasing power.

### Future scenarios
The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, in place since the end of February 2026 after the escalation in the Middle East, has brought the issue back to the top of the national agenda. But for Varese province, high energy costs are not news of these weeks: it is a structural condition that has lasted for four years. The Bank of Italy, in the adverse scenario of the April 2026 economic bulletin, puts 2026 at zero growth and signals the risk of stagflation the following year, identifying precisely the energy pressure as the main channel of transmission of the shock to the Italian economy. The Parliamentary Budget Office, certifying the 2026 maneuver, wrote that the available resources to cushion the blow are already fully committed, reducing the fiscal space compared to 2022. ## Analysis of economic impacts
Italy is structurally more exposed than the European average. Eurostat data shows that Italy imports 73.9% of the energy it consumes, over fifty points above the EU average, and that this share has grown between 2022 and today. The International Energy Agency estimates that in 2025 we will produce 117.8 TWh of electricity from gas, almost three times the EU average — we are the first European country for this source. This means that any shock in gas prices, like the current one, ends up in Italian industrial bills much more directly than in French or German ones.

### Comparison with Europe
The energy cost differential between Italy and major European partners is significant. While Italy pays 278 euros per megawatt-hour, France pays 183, Germany 242, and Spain 171. This gap translates into additional costs for Italian companies, which have to deal with higher energy bills compared to European competitors. For Italian micro and small businesses, those with fewer than fifty employees, which generate over 30% of the country's manufacturing added value, this represents an additional obstacle to competitiveness.

### Impact on cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who work in the province of Varese and reside in Canton Ticino are particularly affected by this situation. The increase in energy costs reflects not only on salaries but also on the cost of living. Local companies, to compensate for higher costs, may be forced to reduce benefits for employees or not renew contracts, negatively influencing the cross-border labor market.

### Mitigation scenarios
To mitigate the impact of high energy costs, companies could consider adopting more energy-efficient technologies or diversifying their sources of supply. However, these solutions require significant investments and time to implement. While waiting for structural solutions, companies and workers must deal with a situation that, according to the Bank of Italy, could last at least until 2027.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for businesses and workers
To tackle the energy crisis, businesses can adopt various strategies. First and foremost, it is essential to conduct a detailed analysis of energy consumption to identify areas for improvement. Adopting more efficient technologies, such as LED lighting systems or high-energy-efficiency machinery, can significantly reduce costs. Additionally, businesses can consider investing in renewable energy sources, such as solar panels or wind farms, to reduce dependence on traditional sources.

### Procedures for applying for incentives
Businesses can also benefit from state and regional incentives for reducing energy consumption. It is important to stay up-to-date with regulations and available funding opportunities. For example, the [incentive calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate potential savings and identify the most suitable incentives for the company's needs.

### Tips for workers
Workers, on the other hand, can adopt more conscious behaviors to reduce energy consumption at home and at work. Small gestures, such as turning off lights when not needed or using low-consumption appliances, can make a difference. Additionally, it is important to monitor the energy market offers to find the most convenient rates.

### Useful tools
To delve into the implications of rising energy costs and find practical solutions, you can use the [energy cost calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows you to estimate the impact of energy costs on the company's budget and identify the most effective strategies to reduce consumption. Furthermore, the [cost analysis](nav:cost-of-living) section provides a detailed overview of energy expenses and how to optimize them.

### Conclusion
The rise in energy costs represents a significant challenge for businesses and workers in the province of Varese. However, with careful planning and the adoption of innovative technologies, it is possible to mitigate the impact of energy costs and ensure long-term economic sustainability.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/28/leggi-notizia/argomenti/economia-13/articolo/limpatto-del-caro-energia-in-provincia-di-varese-una-piccola-impresa-metalmeccanica-paga-il-30.html)*

item.title: Fair Trade Day in Ticino: breakfast events
item.description: On May 9, 2026, Ticino celebrates World Fair Trade Day with breakfasts, tastings, and promotions at various World Shops.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Fair Trade Breakfast in Ticino on May 9, 2026
- Events at various World Shops
- Promotions and tastings of fair trade products

## Key facts
- **What**: World Fair Trade Day
- When: May 9, 2026
- Where: Ticino, Switzerland
- Who: Fair Trade Town, World Shops
- Amount: Up to 20% off on fair trade products

The 'fair trade breakfast' is also in Ticino. On the occasion of 'World Fair Trade Day', celebrated on May 9, 2026, various activities are planned in the canton. From coffee to tea, from bananas to orange juice to flowers: even at breakfast, products from all over the world are consumed, often made under conditions that would not be accepted in Switzerland. Their real cost is not indicated on the label, but falls on people and the environment. This is reported in a note by Fair Trade Town.

In Ticino, Capriasca, Bellinzona and Lugano promote sustainable consumption by involving businesses, catering, schools, institutions and administrations in a local change process. The Fair Trade Town movement counts over 2000 cities in 32 countries; in Switzerland there are 24, three of which are in Ticino.

### Events planned for Saturday, May 9

• Fair Trade Breakfast at the Bellinzona World Shop. You will find coffee, tea, chocolate, biscuits, spices and much more, from 9:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.
• Celebration of 30 years of the Cavergno World Shop and product promotion. From 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.: breakfast in the morning, storytellers at 2:30 p.m. and snack. 10% discount on purchases, world games corner for ages 6 to 99.
• Breakfast product promotion at the Faido World Shop. 20% off on products for a fair trade breakfast: you will find coffee, tea, jams and other selected fair trade products, from 9:00 a.m. to 11:30 a.m.
• Fair Trade Breakfast organized by the Giubiasco World Shop at the Ristorante del Moro. Rich and imaginative breakfast, prepared with fair trade products supplied by the Shop, symbolic path between the Shop and the restaurant and dedicated setup inside the Ristorante del Moro in Giubiasco, from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.
• Tasting of sweet and savory products at the Locarno World Shop and a good coffee: from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.
• Fair and convivial aperitif with quiz at the Lugano World Shop. Delicacies from fair trade and snacks prepared by volunteers from 11:00 a.m.
• Breakfast and tasting of fair trade products at the Mendrisio World Shop. To start the day with taste, breakfast from 9:30 a.m. to 11:00 a.m.
• Fair Solidarity Breakfast organized by the Tesserete World Shop and Fair Trade Town Capriasca at the Irish Pub Club Alpino. Tasty appointment with fair trade products, from 8:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. at via Enrico Fraschina 6 in Tesserete.
• Breakfast with the world organized by the Poschiavo World Shop at the Parish Center. Fair trade breakfast, from 8:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m. Fair trade proposes a different model, based on long-term partnerships, greater transparency, fair wages, and protection of people and the environment. An approach that connects daily choices to global justice, reaffirming that exploitation should not end up on the table.

Organizations in the sector, including members of Swiss Fair Trade, draw attention to these critical issues and the possible alternatives. In Ticino, the Botteghe del Mondo in Bellinzona, Cavergno, Faido, Giubiasco, Locarno, Lugano, Mendrisio, Tesserete, and Poschiavo are ready to welcome visitors with a variety of fair and sustainable products.

### Why participate in fair trade events?
Participating in fair trade events not only allows you to enjoy quality products but also to support a fairer and more sustainable consumption model. By purchasing fair trade products, you contribute to improving the living conditions of producers in developing countries, guaranteeing them fair wages and dignified working conditions.

Moreover, participating in these events is a way to raise awareness among yourself and others about the importance of fair trade and sustainable consumption. The Botteghe del Mondo offer a unique opportunity to discover new products and meet people who share the same values and interests.

### How to participate in fair trade events?
To participate in fair trade events in Ticino, simply visit one of the Botteghe del Mondo listed in the program. It is advisable to check the specific times and participation methods for each event, so you can plan your day better.

Additionally, you can follow the social media pages of the Botteghe del Mondo and Fair Trade Town to stay updated on all the latest news and events. Participating in fair trade events is a simple and concrete way to contribute to a fairer and more sustainable future.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers employed in Ticino, participating in fair trade events can be an opportunity to discover new products and support a more equitable and sustainable consumption model. Additionally, attending these events can be a way to better integrate into the local community and meet new people who share the same values and interests.

### How can cross-border workers benefit from fair trade?

Cross-border workers in Ticino can benefit from fair trade in various ways. By purchasing fair trade products, they can contribute to improving the living conditions of producers in developing countries, ensuring them a fair wage and dignified working conditions. Moreover, participating in fair trade events can be a way to discover new products and support a fairer and more sustainable consumption model.

### What to do if you want to participate in fair trade events?

If you want to participate in fair trade events in Ticino, simply visit one of the World Shops listed in the program. It is advisable to check the specific times and participation methods for each event, so you can plan your day accordingly.

Additionally, you can follow the social media pages of the World Shops and Fair Trade Town to stay updated on all the latest news and upcoming events. Participating in fair trade events is a simple and concrete way to contribute to a fairer and more sustainable future.

For more information on fair trade events in Ticino, you can visit the Fair Trade Town website or contact the participating World Shops directly. Use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how fair trade can influence your monthly budget.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/cronaca/1921439/trade-town-commercio-colazione-svizzera)*

item.title: Vocational training week: 30 events in Ticino
item.description: From May 4 to 8, over 30 events on radio, TV, and in-person to discover professions in Ticino
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Vocational training week from May 4 to 8
- Over 30 events across radio, TV, and in-person
- Teleticino kicks off with a special on SwissSkills 2025

## Key facts
- **What**: Vocational training week
- When: May 4-8, 2024
- Where: Ticino, Switzerland
- Who: Swiss Conference of Vocational Education and Training Offices, SEFRI, DECS Ticino
- Amount: Over 30 events

From May 4 to 8, 2024, the Vocational Training Week returns to Ticino. The initiative, promoted by the Swiss Conference of Vocational Education and Training Offices with the State Secretariat for Education, Research, and Innovation (SEFRI), involves the main radio and television broadcasters in the canton. In Ticino, the Week is coordinated by the Millestrade project of the Department of Education, Culture, and Sport (DECS).

### Special programming
Teleticino symbolically opens the week already on Friday, May 1, with the special 'When I grow up,' which includes a documentary about young people from Italian-speaking Switzerland who are protagonists at SwissSkills 2025. On the radio front, RSI Rete Tre will offer every morning the special edition of 'A life as...', dedicated to five professions told by those who live them firsthand. RSI Rete Uno, on the other hand, will host on Thursday, May 7, in the Casa Svizzera program, the LIFT project, which supports middle school students in approaching the world of work.

### News and participation
Among the novelties of this year, the involvement of Radio Scuola media Besso, which will offer a monographic live broadcast on orientation designed and realized by students. Radio 3i and Radio Ticino will accompany the entire week with interviews and testimonies on the opportunities and challenges of vocational training.

### Events on the territory
In addition to media programming, numerous events are planned on the territory, designed to offer concrete opportunities to discover professions. At the Città dei mestieri della Svizzera italiana, it will be possible to take advantage of tailored meetings, specialist consultations, and informative moments, accompanied by webinars dedicated to higher education opportunities.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The Vocational Training Week represents an important opportunity also for cross-border workers who work in Ticino. The dedicated events and broadcasts offer an opportunity to discover the professions available in the canton and better understand the Swiss education system. This can be particularly useful for those considering moving or finding new employment in Ticino.

### How to participate
To participate in in-person events, it is advisable to consult the websites of the broadcasters involved or the DECS portal for updated information on dates, times, and locations. For those who prefer to follow the events from home, radio and television broadcasts will be available on the channels of the partner broadcasters.

### Conclusions
The Vocational Training Week 2024 in Ticino promises to be an edition rich in content and opportunities. With over 30 events across radio, TV, and in-person, it represents a unique opportunity to discover professions and training opportunities in the canton. For cross-border workers, it is an excellent opportunity to explore new work and training possibilities in Switzerland. ### In-depth analysis
The Professional Training Week is not just an event, but a crucial moment to understand the dynamics of the labor market in Ticino. For cross-border workers, knowing the available professions and training opportunities can make a difference in choosing a new job or moving to Switzerland.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Compared to previous editions, the Professional Training Week 2024 stands out for the involvement of new broadcasters and an even richer program. This reflects the growing importance of professional training in the Canton and the commitment of institutions to promoting job opportunities.

### Practical scenarios
Imagine a cross-border worker who works in Lombardy and is considering moving to Ticino. Participating in the Professional Training Week events would allow them to discover the most in-demand professions, working conditions, and available training opportunities. This could facilitate their decision and help them plan the move better.

### Comparative table

| **Aspect** | 2023 Week | 2024 Week |
|---------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Number of events | Around 20 | Over 30 |
| Broadcasters involved | RSI, Radio Ticino | RSI, Radio Ticino, Radio 3i, Radio Scuola media Besso |
| In-person events | Limited | Numerous |
| Focus | Professional training | Professional training and guidance |

### Practical tips
For cross-border workers interested in participating in the Professional Training Week, it is important to plan ahead. Consulting the websites of the broadcasters involved and the DECS portal can help not to miss the events of interest. Additionally, it is useful to prepare specific questions about professions and training opportunities to maximize the experience.

### Conclusion
The Professional Training Week 2024 in Ticino represents a unique opportunity to discover professions and training opportunities in the Canton. For cross-border workers, it is a precious occasion to explore new job and training possibilities in Switzerland. Participating in the events and following the dedicated broadcasts can make a difference in choosing a new job or moving to Ticino.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate
To participate in the Professional Training Week, you need to follow a few simple but important steps. Here's a practical guide:

1. **Visit the broadcasters' websites**: Check the websites of RSI, Radio Ticino, Radio 3i, and Radio Scuola media Besso for updated information on events and broadcasts.
2. Visit the DECS portal: The Department of Education, Culture and Sport of the Canton of Ticino provides detailed information on in-person events and broadcasts.
3. Plan your participation: Choose the events of interest and plan your participation, taking into account dates, times, and locations.
4. Prepare questions: Prepare specific questions about the professions and training opportunities to maximize your experience.
5. Follow the broadcasts: Follow the dedicated radio and television broadcasts to deepen your knowledge of professions and training opportunities.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to explore job and training opportunities in Ticino, several useful tools are available:

- Salary calculator: To compare salaries in Ticino and Italy and evaluate job opportunities.
- Training guide: To discover the training opportunities available in Ticino and plan your professional path.
- Personalized consultation: To receive specialist advice and detailed information about professions and training opportunities.

### Conclusion
The 2024 Professional Training Week in Ticino is a unique opportunity to discover professions and training opportunities in the Canton. For cross-border workers, participating in events and following dedicated broadcasts can make a difference in choosing a new job or moving to Ticino.
